mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at 5.25% represents restrictive monetary policy. A sustained level above 5.0% reduces equity valuations by increasing the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices equities downward under persistent tightening.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward returns exceeds -0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1528 thousand units represent a late-cycle peak in residential construction. When housing starts exceed 1500 units and begin to decelerate month-over-month, this signals imminent demand contraction. The mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market anticipates the demand shock propagating through construction, materials, and financial sectors.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1500 and month-over-month change turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1528 thousand as of April 2007
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between HOUST deceleration and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.5% combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 137.8 million represents a tight labor market near full employment. When UNRATE remains below 4.7% and PAYEMS growth slows below 100k per month, this signals wage pressure and reduced hiring momentum. The mechanism predicts negative equity returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices inflation risk and earnings growth deceleration.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% and PAYEMS at 137831k as of May 2007
failure_condition: The joint signal (UNRATE < 4.7% AND PAYEMS growth < 100k) fails to predict negative returns with Rank IC > -0.08, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 112.69 represents mid-cycle supply capacity utilization. When INDPRO growth decelerates to below 0.2% month-over-month while CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated above 207, this signals stagflationary pressure: constrained supply growth with persistent inflation. The mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as real earnings margins compress.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 112.69 and CPIAUCSL at 207.387 as of May 2007
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to produce negative returns with Rank IC > -0.06 over any 2-year window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 377.9 billion dollars represent consumer demand strength. When RSAFS growth decelerates to below 0.3% month-over-month, this signals weakening consumer spending momentum. Combined with elevated FEDFUNDS at 5.25%, the mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices near-term earnings expectations downward in response to demand weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 377885 million as of May 2007
failure_condition: RSAFS deceleration below 0.3% fails to predict negative 5-day returns with Rank IC > -0.04, or SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The spread between FEDFUNDS (5.25%) and the implicit real rate embedded in CPIAUCSL inflation (207.387 index level) indicates real monetary tightness. When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% and CPI inflation remains above 2.5% annualized, real rates are restrictive. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days if the Fed begins to signal rate cuts, as the market anticipates monetary accommodation. The trigger is a Fed communication indicating pause or easing intent.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS held steady for 2+ consecutive months while inflation remains elevated
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% and CPIAUCSL at 207.387 as of May 2007
failure_condition: The pause signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.05, or SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances where FEDFUNDS is held constant.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 137.8 million represents cumulative labor market strength. When PAYEMS growth turns negative month-over-month (indicating job losses), this signals an economic downturn. The mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings downward in anticipation of recession-driven profit contraction and reduced consumer spending.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137831 thousand as of May 2007
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and recession onset
failure_condition: Negative PAYEMS growth fails to predict negative 60-day returns with Rank IC > -0.10, or SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances following PAYEMS declines.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 11531.7 billion chained 2017 dollars combined with HOUST at 1528 thousand units indicates late-cycle demand saturation. When GDPC1 growth slows to below 2.0% annualized and HOUST begins declining, this signals demand exhaustion. The mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward and anticipates earnings deceleration across cyclical sectors.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% and HOUST month-over-month change negative
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11531.7 billion as of Q1 2007 and HOUST at 1528 thousand as of April 2007
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with Rank IC > -0.07, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances where both conditions are met.
